headline: The harness around the model, context, memory, evals, and durable logs, is the cycle's dominant thread in agentic engineering.
body: Cole Medin breaks down Google's agentic engineering whitepaper, arguing the model is only 10% of success while the surrounding harness of context, tools, guardrails, and verification is the critical 90%, and framing AI coding as a spectrum from vibe coding to orchestration [0]. The AI Engineer talks converge on the same point from production: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa details the core agent loop, evals, long context, and monitoring behind OG Assist [3]; Victor Savkin frames coding agents as a genius with amnesia and proposes Nx and Polygraph for persistent context [4]; Ishaan Sehgal argues durable session logs become the core primitive as models commoditize [6]; and Nishant Gupta of Meta covers continuous online evaluation, human-in-the-loop, and drift detection at scale [11]. Angie Jones reframes the work as building systems rather than code [12], while Google Cloud's data agents ship via ADK and MCP across Gemini CLI, Claude Code, and Codex [10], and DeepReinforce's open Ornith models bring self-scaffolded agentic coding to local 9B and 35B deployments [1]. On the frontier, separate videos track Anthropic's Fable: Mike Krieger discusses building AI-native products with it [13], while another speculates on a Fable 5 return from leaked strings and Bedrock listings [5].

cited_items.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
